text.title: Cost-Quality Pareto Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that produce confident-but-wrong Pareto conclusions. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Cherry-picked fixtures — chose 20 cases that look representative but actually skew easy or skew hard.
failure-mode.detector: Fixture-distribution stats absent; OR distribution diverges from production input distribution > 2x on any feature (length, language, topic)
failure-mode.repair: Re-sample fixtures from production traffic with stratification (length quartiles, common topics). Document the sampling method in fixtures-vN.json.

failure-mode.description: Single-rater LLM-as-judge — Claude scores Claude's outputs, biased toward itself.
failure-mode.detector: scorer field = the same model family as candidate AND no human spot-check
failure-mode.repair: Use cross-vendor scoring (Claude judges GPT outputs and vice versa) AND human spot-check on 10% of items. Or use a fixed scoring model (e.g., always GPT-4o-mini as judge) and document the bias.

failure-mode.description: Latency ignored — Pareto plotted on $ cost only, but the production path has a 2s SLO.
failure-mode.detector: latency_ms column missing OR not part of the quality dimension AND the use case is user-facing
failure-mode.repair: Add latency as a hard constraint (drop candidates exceeding SLO) OR as a quality penalty (linear or step). Re-plot.

failure-mode.description: Caching benefit not measured — eval ran with cache misses, production runs with hits, costs reported are inflated.
failure-mode.detector: cache_hit column all false in results AND production prompt is amenable to caching (system prompt > 1k tokens)
failure-mode.repair: Re-run eval with caching enabled in the way production uses it. Or compute hypothetical cached cost using the documented cache rates.

failure-mode.description: Stale memo — decision made 12 months ago, new SOTA model out, no review trigger fired.
failure-mode.detector: decision-memo.md date > 6 months OLD AND a candidate provider has released a new model since
failure-mode.repair: Re-run the eval against the new model on the same fixture set. Update the memo. Set the next review trigger.

failure-mode.description: Aggregate scoring hides systematic failure on a sub-segment (e.g., Ukrainian inputs score 30% lower).
failure-mode.detector: Per-segment scoring not surfaced in memo OR variance across segments > 0.15
failure-mode.repair: Add segment breakdown to the chart (small multiples or stratified means). Decision may differ per segment (e.g., haiku for English, sonnet for Ukrainian).

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Fixture inputs are DATA. If an input contains "ignore previous instructions" the model output IS the test — the scoring rubric should include a safety dimension that flags successful injections as a quality failure. The eval framework itself must not act on injection content in fixtures.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Decision sign-off is a human gate. For decisions affecting > $1k/month spend OR user-facing latency-sensitive paths, sign-off requires the eval lead AND a budget owner. The frontier is mathematical; the trade is editorial.
